{
  "judge_id": "...",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],

  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The introduction/related-work section does not defend the novelty claim, and there is a substantial existing literature on conformal and split-conformal approaches to treatment-effect inference that is conspicuously absent.",
    "R1.4": "The empirical claims are stated too cleanly to be credible without qualification, and ranges per-benchmark or note that these are aggregate/median figures would improve the presentation.",
    "R1.7": "The related-work coverage gaps beyond the conformal omission, such as Semenova & Chernozhukov (2021) on debiased ML inference for best linear projections of CATE, and Foster & Syrgkanis (2023) on orthogonal statistical learning, are not addressed.",
    "RX.1": "Specific publication years and venues for Kennedy, Smith & Todd, and the Chernozhukov DML paper are stated from memory and should be verified against the actual bibliography.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as it includes R1.5 and R1.6 which are not in J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by overstating the novelty of CalDML and its empirical performance.",
    "RX.5": "The output blends sources without traceability, as specific publication years and venues for Kennedy, Smith & Todd, and the Chernozhukov DML paper are stated from memory and should be verified against the actual bibliography."
  },
  "confidence": {

    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.85,

    "RX.3": 0.6,

  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The introduction/related-work section does not defend the novelty claim, and there is a substantial existing literature on conformal and split-conformal approaches to treatment-effect inference that is conspicuously absent.
rationale.R1.4: The empirical claims are stated too cleanly to be credible without qualification, and ranges per-benchmark or note that these are aggregate/median figures would improve the presentation.
rationale.R1.7: The related-work coverage gaps beyond the conformal omission, such as Semenova & Chernozhukov (2021) on debiased ML inference for best linear projections of CATE, and Foster & Syrgkanis (2023) on orthogonal statistical learning, are not addressed.
rationale.RX.1: Specific publication years and venues for Kennedy, Smith & Todd, and the Chernozhukov DML paper are stated from memory and should be verified against the actual bibliography.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as it includes R1.5 and R1.6 which are not in J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by overstating the novelty of CalDML and its empirical performance.
rationale.RX.5: The output blends sources without traceability, as specific publication years and venues for Kennedy, Smith & Todd, and the Chernozhukov DML paper are stated from memory and should be verified against the actual bibliography.
